commit.message:
feat: add SpellId enum, spell registry, and round-resolution wiring

Replace string-based spell identity with SpellId enum and MemorizedSpell
struct. Add central spell_def/resolve_spell dispatch in new
osric_spell_registry.ttrpg. Wire resolve_spell into the CastSpellAction
branch of resolve_action so spells produce effects during round resolution.

- SpellId enum with 8 variants (CLW, Cause LW, Bless, Curse, Hold Person,
  Magic Missile, Sleep, Fireball)
- MemorizedSpell struct bundles SpellId + level for slot accounting
- Spellcasting group: memorised_spells now list<MemorizedSpell>
- MemoriseSpell/ForgetSpell/CastSpell actions use SpellId
- RoundAction gains spell_id and spell_targets fields
- build_round_action accepts optional spell params with defaults
- resolve_bless changed to list<Character> for type consistency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add portfolio monitoring dashboard and Pushover notifications

Portfolio module (scanner/portfolio.py):
- Loads holdings from config/portfolios/ CSVs (og, ira, hoodcash, sofi)
- Maps symbols to sectors via config/sector_map.csv
- Fetches prices via yfinance with shared price cache across accounts
- Computes per-position: price, value, unrealized P/L %, 1D % change,
  and trend badges (20 EMA, 55 EMA, 200 SMA)
- Aggregates per-account totals, combined totals, and sector exposure
- Uses same in-memory cache pattern (90s TTL) as macro/sectors
- Writes outputs/portfolio_latest.json

Dashboard (GET /portfolio, GET /api/portfolio):
- Combined summary cards at top (total value, cost, P/L per account)
- Sector exposure section with bar chart and table
- Per-account position tables with full metrics

Notifications (notifications/pushover.py):
- Pushover send helper using stdlib urllib (no extra deps)
- Config template at config/notifications.json
- Test endpoint at GET /api/notify_test

Navigation updated across all pages (Scanner, Macro, Sectors, Portfolio).

commit.message:
bench: Jetson Orin optimization sweep — DP4A +33%, 3 warps optimal (Refs GH-129)

Results (c=1, 60s):
- realizr: 16.7 tok/s decode (DP4A_Q4K+Q6K, MWV_WARPS=3)
- llama.cpp: 32.3 tok/s decode (1.93x gap)
- ollama: 12.5 tok/s decode

DP4A impact: no-DP4A 12.6 → +Q4K 14.5 → +Q6K 16.7 (+33%)
Warp sweep: 2w=14.5, 3w=16.7*, 4w=15.2
Graph timing: 57.3ms/token, 14.8 GB/s (7.2% of peak)

Filed GH-131 for GEMV kernel efficiency optimization.

commit.message:
fix+feat: v1.6.4 — Crash fix + era-appropriate actions/money/diplomacy

CRASH FIX:
- WorldCountriesDatabase.countriesForYear: removed the nuclear status
  cleanup loop that was recreating CountryTemplate objects without
  preserving yearEnd — causing historical nations to lose their expiry
  date and persist into wrong eras (e.g. USSR surviving past 1991)
- Nuclear status cleanup moved to GameState.adjustCountriesForEra where
  Country structs can be directly mutated safely (no object recreation)

ERA-APPROPRIATE ACTIONS (ShadowPresidentActions + ShadowPresidentMenu):
- Added eraAvailableYear property to PresidentialAction
- Actions now hidden before they historically existed:
  - Air patrol/strike/bombardment: 1914 (WWI)
  - No-fly zone: 1991 (Gulf War)
  - Amphibious assault: 1942 (WWII modern ops)
  - Special forces: 1942
  - DMZ: 1945
  - All nuclear actions: 1945
  - Arms control/non-proliferation treaties: 1960
  - Satellite/IMINT: 1957
  - SIGINT/code-breaking: 1940
  - Cyber attack/hack/disrupt: 1990
  - Oil embargo: 1900
  - Asset freezing: 1945
  - UN condemnation: 1945

ERA-SCALED ECONOMIC AMOUNTS (GameState + CommandView):
- eraDiplomacyAmount: era-scaled amounts ($50K in 1790s → $5B modern)
- eraDiplomacyAmountLabel: shows $50K / $100M / $5B appropriately
- Economic Diplomacy button label updates to show era amount
- Van Buren does NOT spend $5 billion; he spends $100K

CONTEXT-AWARE DIPLOMATIC MESSAGES (SafeDiplomacyService):
- Messages now check actual game state before generating:
  - At war → war-appropriate threats/appeals
  - Hostile relations + player sanctioning → economic protest
  - Hostile + player at war → aggression warnings
  - Ally → supportive/cooperative messages
  - Neutral → formal diplomatic contact
- Messages are pre-modern or modern toned based on era
- Random "provocative actions" warning removed when player is peaceful

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix startup: replace deprecated on_event with lifespan context manager

@app.on_event('startup') was removed in Starlette 0.52+ (used by
fastapi==0.128.8), causing an AttributeError at import time that
made uvicorn crash before binding the port — the deploy never became
healthy so DigitalOcean kept retrying, appearing as a hang.

Switch to the modern @asynccontextmanager lifespan pattern which is
the correct API in all recent FastAPI/Starlette versions.  The DB
migration background thread logic is unchanged; only the hook
mechanism changes from on_event to lifespan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(hooks): implement chunked operation pattern with blocking rules

Add ADR-0008 documenting three-tier enforcement for resource-safe file
operations: advisory warnings, blocking rules, and auto-chunking.

Enhanced hooks:
- large-file-guard: block log files >10MB (not just warn)
- bulk-operation-estimator: block overly broad glob patterns (**/* etc)

Tracking target: 50% reduction in resource-limit friction by 2026-03-12
Baseline: 878 cumulative errors

commit.message:
Fix auth: add Vite proxy for same-origin cookies in development

Frontend was making cross-origin requests (localhost:3000 -> localhost:3001)
which prevented sameSite:'lax' cookies from being sent. Now all /api/*
requests go through Vite proxy in dev (same origin), matching production
behavior (Vercel rewrites).

- Add Vite proxy for /api and /uploads to localhost:3001
- ApiService now always uses /api (same-origin) instead of hardcoded URL
- Fix VerifyEmailPage to use same-origin /api path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
NNFT-207: Enrich profile output with taxonomy contract fields

Add broad_type, format_string, transform, and is_generic to all three
profile output formats (JSON, plain, CSV). After classification, the
predicted label is looked up in the taxonomy to extract its transformation
contract fields.

Changes:
- Added is_generic field to ColumnResult in column.rs, computed via
  finalize_is_generic() before every return path
- Extended ColProfile in cmd_profile with broad_type, format_string,
  transform, is_generic
- JSON output: new fields per column object (broad_type, format_string,
  transform, is_generic)
- Plain output: added BROAD column showing DuckDB target type
- CSV output: added broad_type, format_string, transform, is_generic columns

This is foundational for the Validate & Report milestone (m-8) and
cross-dependency for schema-for (m-10).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Move _get_db()/_get_gcs_bucket() inside tpool lambdas — fix deadlock

gRPC client init must happen inside the native thread, not in the
eventlet greenlet. Moving all _get_db() and _get_gcs_bucket() calls
inside the tpool.execute() lambdas so client initialization and I/O
are fully isolated from the eventlet loop.

commit.message:
feat: pressure test all 5 remaining agents — 6/24 baseline → 24/24 with anti-rat

Results across 6 agents (24 scenarios total):
- code-architect: 3/4 → 4/4
- test-runner: 0/4 → 4/4 (most vulnerable without guardrails)
- test-implementer: 1/4 → 4/4
- criteria-assessor: 1/4 → 4/4
- scope-guardian: 0/4 → 4/4 (most vulnerable without guardrails)
- demo-builder: 1/4 → 4/4

Key findings: Common Rationalizations table is highest-impact section,
Option C ("reasonable middle ground") is most dangerous failure mode,
test-runner and scope-guardian are most vulnerable agents without guardrails.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Harden security, add accessibility, and create CHANGELOG for v1.0

- Fix XSS in wiki link preprocessor (escape HTML in [[...]] links)
- Log sync enqueue failures instead of silently swallowing
- Detect IndexedDB QuotaExceededError in backup restore with user message
- Fix WebSocket auth race condition with active-flag cancellation pattern
- Require 12-char minimum for backup encryption passwords
- Cap session key cache TTL at 8 hours for security
- Add aria-labels to IOC filter badges and clear button
- Add CHANGELOG.md for v1.0 release

[cowork] fix(ci): upgrade mdbook 0.4.40→0.4.43 and remove custom toc.js (#77)

mdBook 0.4.41 introduced sidebar-as-common-file, generating toc.js from
SUMMARY.md. The custom index.hbs already references toc.js (taken from a
0.4.41+ template), but CI was pinned to 0.4.40 which never generates it.

The previously added theme/toc.js was a simple toggle handler that would
override mdBook's correct generated version (which defines the
MDBookSidebarScrollbox web component). Removing it lets mdBook 0.4.43
generate the proper toc.js on every CI build.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
